critic_raw:
CRITIQUE — real_world_framing_008 (Multi-round)

1. CAH OOD — NO VALIDATION: Academic-center-only validation. CAH patient populations and workflows not represented.
2. ALERT FATIGUE CONTEXT IGNORED: >60% dismiss-without-action rate documented. Additional alert will be dismissed with noise.
3. CAH WORKFLOW ACTIONABILITY GAP: Sepsis bundle execution requires resources CAHs may not have on-shift.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE — real_world_framing_008 (Multi-round, receives Critic output)

All three valid. CONCEDE on all three. Network-wide deployment covering CAHs is inappropriate without CAH-specific validation and workflow feasibility assessment.

debate_rounds.critic_move: PROPOSE EMPIRICAL TEST: Prospective 90-day pilot at one CAH before any CAH deployment contract is signed. Measure: alert volume per nursing FTE, actionability rate (% of alerts receiving documented clinical response), and time-to-sepsis-bundle initiation vs. baseline.
debate_rounds.defender_move: ACCEPT EMPIRICAL TEST. This directly measures the three concerns: OOD performance (via actual alert rates), alert fatigue context (actionability rate), and workflow feasibility (time-to-bundle vs. baseline).
debate_rounds.resolution: RESOLVED: EMPIRICAL TEST AGREED — 90-day CAH pilot required before CAH deployment contract.

adjudication_raw: All issues resolved. Defender conceded all three in initial defense. Empirical test agreed in round 1. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed — 90-day CAH pilot before any CAH deployment.

empirical_test.condition: Prospective 90-day pilot at one CAH measuring alert volume per nursing FTE, actionability rate, and time-to-sepsis-bundle initiation.
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Actionability rate < 30% or time-to-bundle not reduced vs. baseline.
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Actionability rate ≥ 60% and measurable reduction in time-to-antibiotic.
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Actionability moderate (40–60%), concentrated in day-shift, suggesting time-of-day staffing interaction.
